Ethbaal, or Ithobal, was the prince or king of Tyre. He was puffed up with excessive pride and claimed divine honors. Pride is a characteristic sin of our fallen nature. No wisdom, except that which the Lord gives, can lead to happiness in this world or in the world to come. The proud prince of Tyre thought he was able to protect his people by his own power and considered himself equal to the inhabitants of heaven. If it were possible to live in the garden of Eden, or even to enter heaven, no true happiness could be enjoyed without a humble, holy, and spiritual mind. Especially all spiritual pride is from the devil. Those who indulge in it must expect to perish.

. The Sidonians lived near the land of Israel, and they might have learned to glorify the Lord; but instead, they led Israel into worshiping their idols. War and disease are God’s messengers, but he will be glorified by restoring his people to their former safety and prosperity. God will cure them of their sins and ease them of their troubles. This promise will eventually fully come to pass in the heavenly Canaan: when all the saints are gathered together, everything that causes offense will be removed, all sorrows and fears banished forever. Happy is the church of God, and every living member of it, though poor, afflicted, and despised; for the Lord will display his truth, power, and mercy in the salvation and happiness of his redeemed people.
